title: JSON Value
description: Single JSON value - can be object, string, number, boolean, or null
documentation:
## Purpose
The most permissive JSON type, representing any valid JSON value at the top level: object, array, string, number, boolean, or null. Use this when the shape of the JSON is unknown or intentionally unconstrained.

## When to use
Use `media:json;textable` when a node may produce or consume JSON of varying or unknown structure. When the structure is known to be an object, prefer [JSON Object](https://capdag.com/browse/media:json;textable;record). When it is an array, prefer [JSON Array](https://capdag.com/browse/media:json;textable;list).

## Notes
Nodes that accept this type must be prepared to handle any JSON value type. Consumers that require a specific shape should validate the payload against the expected structure before processing.
